rationale: A file named 'kxwn.lock' was created in AppData\Roaming\Microsoft by certutil.exe (EV-001). Subsequently, rundll32.exe executed this 'kxwn.lock' file (A0), which then initiated an outbound network connection to a private IP address on port 443 (EV-002). This sequence of events strongly suggests the execution of malicious code and subsequent command and control communication.
